preamble: The shift from the back unrounded vowel [ʌ] to the front unrounded vowel [æ] (or its close relative [a]) is a stark example of vowel fronting. It often occurs as part of a chain shift where the front of the mouth 'empties out', pulling back vowels forward.

examples.note: As part of the California Vowel Shift, the STRUT vowel moves forward to fill the space vacated by other front vowels.
The STRUT vowel is heavily fronted, often sounding like the American 'ash' vowel to outsiders.
